inline_comments.message: Why are you changing the version like this?

These changes will break unit tests and other functionality that needs to be changed in SO, so I was hoping to have a separate SNAPSHOT version so that I can then update SO with the new version and make changes to fix issues caused by this change. Is this versioning pattern not correct, or is this not the correct way to change the version? Should it be something like 1.6.3-SNAPSHOT?

If you need to bump use 1.6.3-SNAPSHOT not 1.6.2.0-SNAPSHOT.

I think version.properties always need to be kept in synch with the pom files. One time I didn't do this and the release build got very messed up.

Normally we do a release before rolling a new snapshot. Not sure if skipping the release causes any harm.

Okay, sure I'll update to 1.6.3-SNAPSHOT instead. I wasn't sure if this was the right way to increase the version because, as you mentioned, we haven't had a 1.6.2 release yet. I'll update version.properties as well.
